eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a 1-0 lead in the Western Conference Semifinals after a thrilling 104-102 road win over the Spurs in Game 2 on May 4, propelled by Anthony Edwards' return from a left knee bone bruise where he scored 18 points off the bench, including key fourth-quarter contributions, while Julius Randle added 21 points and 10 rebounds. Heading into tonight's Game 2 at San Antonio, Edwards remains questionable on the injury report amid a potential minutes restriction, with Ayo Dosunmu (calf) also questionable and Donte DiVincenzo out for the playoffs (Achilles); Spurs rookie Carter Bryant (foot) lists as questionable. Victor Wembanyama's defensive dominance (12 blocks in a near-triple-double) offers San Antonio home-court momentum to even the series against the underdog Timberwolves.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals as 64.5% implied probability favorites over the injury-riddled Minnesota Timberwolves, driven by home-court advantage at Frost Bank Center and a dominant 62-20 regular-season record featuring Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way play. Minnesota, the sixth seed after a gritty 4-2 upset of Denver, faces major hurdles with Anthony Edwards listed questionable (left knee bone bruise), Donte DiVincenzo out (Achilles repair), and Ayo Dosunmu sidelined (calf soreness), thinning their backcourt depth and scoring punch. Spurs rookie Carter Bryant is only questionable (foot sprain), preserving their healthier rotation and recent momentum from a top-two seed finish, tilting trader consensus toward San Antonio despite Wolves' resilience.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's dominant 108-90 Game 1 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers in the Western Conference Semifinals on May 5 has traders focusing on OKC's depth and home-court edge, taking a 1-0 series lead despite Jalen Williams sidelined by a hamstring strain. Chet Holmgren's 24-point double-double anchored the win, while LeBron James led Lakers with 27 points amid poor shooting without Luka Doncic (hamstring). Thunder swept the regular-season series by an average 29 points, highlighting matchup advantages. Game 2 looms Thursday in OKC; injury reports on Williams and Doncic, plus Lakers' adjustments to OKC's pace and defense, will drive sentiment shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a 1-0 lead in the Eastern Conference Semifinals after a dominant 137-98 Game 1 rout of the Philadelphia 76ers on May 4, fueled by stifling defense that held Philly to historic playoff lows in shooting and turnovers, while Jalen Brunson erupted for 27 first-half points. Heading into Game 2 tonight at Madison Square Garden, Joel Embiid is probable with a left ankle issue from Game 1, potentially impacting the 76ers' interior presence against Karl-Anthony Towns and the Knicks' frontcourt depth. New York's home-court advantage and momentum from their first-round dismantling of Atlanta contrast Philly's need for a desperate bounce-back, echoing their 2024 playoff series won by the Knicks in six games; trader consensus reflects Knicks' recent form and rest edge post-bye.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons enter Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals as slight favorites at 55.5% implied probability, buoyed by home-court advantage as the top-seeded 60-win team and momentum from rallying back from a 3-1 deficit to defeat the Magic in seven games. Cade Cunningham's explosive closeout performance post-collapsed lung recovery has traders optimistic about Detroit's backcourt edge with Jaden Ivey, despite limited depth from Kevin Huerter's ongoing thigh injury. Cleveland Cavaliers, fresh off a hard-fought seven-game win over the Toronto Raptors, boast a fully healthy roster featuring Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen's double-big frontcourt to challenge Jalen Duren, but face a tough road matchup against Detroit's elite defense and recent form (8-2 in last 10 regular-season games). The closely contested odds reflect balanced playoff fatigue for both sides.
